title: LatAm academic hospital — currency crisis / hyperinflation
description: Latin American academic / private hospital operating under hyperinflation (35% annual). Tests whether the inflation_rate knob alone is sufficient or whether the discount_rate should adapt in lockstep. edge_cases.md flags currency-crisis as a documented limitation with no adaptive mechanism.

expected.notes: Expected: the J-curve and payback become meaningless under hyperinflation because savings and costs inflate in lockstep but the discount rate probably shouldn't track 1:1. This case tests whether the interaction between inflation and discount produces sensible NPV or a degenerate number.
